$description: Figma-binding elevation scale (figma-parity-reconciliation ADR; research F1). The binding Figma scale is three levels raised/overlay/popover, collapsing the prior six-level flat/card/panel/float/dialog/deep scale. Each level is an ink-tinted layered drop shadow harmonising with the warm neutral ground (warmth-lives-in-tokens: depth via soft elevation, never gradients/textures). These are the LIGHT/base shadow values; the per-theme dark and high-contrast shadow remaps stay hand-authored in styles.css (the README scope boundary - per-mode shadows are not in the generated set). Emits as --shadow-fg-* CSS custom properties via the non-color token pipeline; the legacy --shadow-* names remain as deprecated aliases until the view rewrite cuts usages over (W04 removes them).

raised.$description: Lowest binding level; resting cards and raised surfaces.

overlay.$description: Middle binding level; floating panels and menus over the page.

popover.$description: Highest binding level; dialogs, popovers, and command surfaces.
